line: GROVE_RESULT
Blocked on repository-wide compile failures outside this bead, but the hydration parity work is partially implemented.
GROVE_ARTIFACTS
- Updated `crates/membrain-cli/src/main.rs:7273` so `membrain daemon` now passes the same DB paths as MCP via `with_db_paths(...)`.
- Updated `crates/membrain-daemon/src/daemon.rs:357` startup hydration to load persisted runtime records instead of re-encoding CLI rows.
- Updated `crates/membrain-daemon/src/daemon.rs:1474` / `1504` to convert between persisted runtime records and in-memory runtime records.
- Updated `crates/membrain-daemon/src/daemon.rs:1539` to persist daemon runtime memories back to SQLite on encode/store.
- Added daemon restart parity tests in `crates/membrain-daemon/src/daemon.rs:8415`.
- Added CLI restart parity test in `crates/membrain-cli/tests/cli_end_to_end.rs:67`.
GROVE_LESSONS
- There were two concrete parity gaps:
1. `membrain daemon` from CLI was not wiring DB paths, while `membrain mcp` was.
2. daemon startup was replaying persisted rows by re-encoding instead of hydrating canonical persisted runtime records.
- Validation is currently dominated by unrelated active breakage in `membrain-core`, so local bead progress must be reported separately from repo health.
GROVE_DECISIONS
- Kept degraded explain signaling explicit for non-exact query explain paths.
- Did not touch unrelated failing `membrain-core` files because they overlap other active work and are outside this bead’s reserved slice.
- Coordinated via Agent Mail and acknowledged incoming messages.
GROVE_WARNINGS
- `cargo check --all-targets` is currently failing due to unrelated existing errors in:
- `crates/membrain-core/src/engine/result.rs`
- `crates/membrain-core/src/health.rs`
- Because of that, targeted test execution could not complete.
- `crates/membrain-daemon/src/daemon.rs` was being edited concurrently during this task, so patches were applied incrementally against moving file contents.
GROVE_CHECKPOINT
